metadata.author: John of Damascus: Exposition of the Orthodox Faith

ccel.title: NPNF2-09. Hilary of Poitiers, John of Damascus

source.title: NPNF2-09. Hilary of Poitiers, John of Damascus

title: Concerning ignorance and servitude.

source.title: NPNF2-09. Hilary of Poitiers, John of Damascus

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: He assumed, it is to be noted, the ignorant and servile nature.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: For it is man’s nature to be the servant of God, his Creator, and he does not possess knowledge of the future.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: If, then, as Gregory the Theologian holds, you are to separate the realm of sight from the realm of thought, the flesh is to be spoken of as both servile and ignorant, but on account of the identity of subsistence and the inseparable union the soul of the Lord was enriched with the knowledge of the future as also with the other miraculous powers.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: For just as the flesh of men is not in its own nature life-giving, while the flesh of our Lord which was united in subsistence with God the Word Himself, although it was not exempt from the mortality of its nature, yet became life-giving through its union in subsistence with the Word, and we may not say that it was not and is not for ever life-giving: in like manner His human nature does not in essence possess the knowledge of the future, but the soul of the Lord through its union with God the Word Himself and its identity in subsistence was enriched, as I said, with the knowledge of the future as well as with the other miraculous powers.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: Observe further that we may not speak of Him as servant.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: For the words servitude and mastership are not marks of nature but indicate relationship, to something, such as that of fatherhood and sonship.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: For these do not signify essence but relation.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: It is just as we said, then, in connection with ignorance, that if you separate with subtle thoughts, that is, with fine imaginings, the created from the uncreated, the flesh is a servant, unless it has been united with God the Word.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: But how can it be a servant when it is once united in subsistence?

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: For since Christ is one, He cannot be His own servant and Lord.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: For these are not simple predications but relative.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: Whose servant, then could He be?

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: His Father’s?

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: The Son, then, would not have all the Father’s attributes, if He is the Father’s servant and yet in no respect His own.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: Besides, how could the apostle say concerning us who were adopted by Him, So that you are no longer a servant but a son, if indeed He is Himself a servant?

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: The word servant, then, is used merely as a title, though not in the strict meaning: but for our sakes He assumed the form of a servant and is called a servant among us.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: For although He is without passion, yet for our sake He was the servant of passion and became the minister of our salvation.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: Those, then, who say that He is a servant divide the one Christ into two, just as Nestorius did.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: But we declare Him to be Master and Lord of all creation, the one Christ, at once God and man, and all-knowing.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: For in Him are all the treasures of wisdom and knowledge, the hidden treasures.
